briefing.incidentSummary:
Twelve students at a Mumbai engineering college suddenly show 100% attendance — despite multiple verified absences.

The college attendance portal was accessed and records altered over three nights.

An inside job is suspected.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Identify who altered the attendance records

› Find which credentials were used

› Collect at least 3 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Tanmay D
suspects.profileNotes: CS student with SQL knowledge. Accessed the attendance database directly using stolen faculty credentials. Charged 500 rupees per student for the service.

suspects.name: Prof. Mehta
suspects.profileNotes: Credentials were stolen from Prof. Mehta's unattended workstation. Professor had no knowledge of the breach.

suspects.name: Rekha P
suspects.profileNotes: Has portal access but no motive. Activity confined to business hours. No nighttime logins from her credentials.

suspects.name: Rohan S
suspects.profileNotes: Was one of the 12 beneficiaries — paid Tanmay for the service. Did not perform the hack himself.

items.label: Need attendance fixed? ₹500 per subject. DM me.
items.detail: Posted in a college WhatsApp group 4 days before the breach. Direct solicitation for the illegal service.

items.label: Done bro. Check portal tomorrow morning.
items.detail: Sent to 12 different students over 3 nights after each batch of changes was made.

items.label: Sending ₹500 now. Change my attendance for Networks subject.
items.detail: Rohan S was a customer — not the perpetrator. Shows demand existed but does not link to the actual breach.

items.label: attendance_db_access_log.txt
items.detail: Database access log shows Prof. Mehta's credentials used at 1:14 AM, 1:22 AM, and 2:07 AM across 3 nights. Professor was asleep — credentials stolen.

items.label: executed_queries.sql
items.detail: 36 UPDATE queries executed on student attendance table. All follow the same pattern: UPDATE attendance SET present=1 WHERE student_id=XXXX. Written with beginner-level SQL — consistent with a CS student, not a professional.

hiddenItem.label: hostel_wifi_log.txt
hiddenItem.detail: Hostel WiFi log shows device TANMAY-LAPTOP connected from Block C at exactly the same times as the database intrusions. MAC address confirmed to Tanmay D's laptop.

minigame.title: Decode Hostel Network Log
minigame.hint: Caesar shift -3. Whose device was on the hostel network?
hints: It is a student's name followed by their initial.

onSuccess.message: hostel_wifi_log.txt is now available in your file feed.

items.label: Stolen Credential Source
items.detail: Prof. Mehta's credentials were captured via a keylogger installed on the faculty room computer 6 days before the breach. Keylogger file matches software found on Tanmay D's USB drive.

items.label: Intrusion Source IP
items.detail: IP 10.0.1.77 originates from the college hostel network — Block C, Room 312. That room is assigned to Tanmay D.

timeline.title: Keylogger Installed
timeline.description: Keylogger placed on Prof. Mehta's faculty PC

timeline.title: Service Advertised
timeline.description: Tanmay posts offer in college WhatsApp group

timeline.title: Students Verify Changes
timeline.description: 12 students confirm their attendance is now 100%

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Tanmay D exposed as the attendance hacker.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: The real culprit was Tanmay D.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

aria.welcomeToHub:
...Twelve students. Perfect attendance. All lies.
Somebody sold shortcuts into the system.
Find who opened that door.
aria.exploreFeeds:
Database logs. SQL queries. Hostel network traces.
The breach happened at night. So did the money.
aria.viewEvidence:
Stolen credentials. Beginner SQL. Hostel IP.
This was an inside job by someone who knew just enough.
aria.decryptionHint:
The hostel network log has the device name encoded.
Decode it.
aria.flagSuspect:
A trace is forming.
You're on to something — keep pulling the thread.
More fragments remain. Don't stop now.
